methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $28M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Radian Aerospace is a Seed-stage space & aerospace company that has raised $27.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in the sector, including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Of three triangulation methods evaluated, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses total capital raised ($27.5M) as a proxy for current valuation and applies a typical Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x, yielding an implied valuation of $69M. Peer-Median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace sector—only 0 datapoints met narrow filtering criteria. Public-Comp Anchoring was inapplicable because the target's revenue is not disclosed; while the 5 public comps trade at a median P/S of 25.8x, this multiple cannot be applied without a known revenue figure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $58M to $79M, centered on $69M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the Stage-Progression implied value to account for execution risk and market timing. Key uncertainties include the lack of revenue disclosure (which would enable public-comp validation) and the absence of sufficiently granular private peer data at identical stage and scale. A revenue-based refinement or emergence of additional Seed-stage aerospace comparables would strengthen confidence in this valuation.
